field_values.action_summary: A three-day period where Dorothy and Oz work together to construct a large green silk balloon. They apply glue to make it airtight, attach a basket, and prepare a heating mechanism using wood provided by the Tin Woodman.
field_values.emotional_beat: From heavy labor to focused anticipation.
field_values.camera_intent: Montage/Insert shots (glue, silk, wood); Wide shots (balloon growth over time); Medium shots (dorothy and oz tandem work)
field_values.end_state: scene resolves into the next narrative beat
movement_notes: Incremental size and state of the green silk balloon
Weather conditions shifting across three days
field_values.transition_notes:
# Scene Contract: CH017_SC002

## Production Intent
A three-day montage documenting the technical construction of a large green silk balloon. The sequence must visually communicate the progression from raw materials to a functional flight apparatus through increasing scale and assembly complexity.

## Required Cast
- dorothy (weary traveler, white dress/clothing)
- oz (the wizard, humbug persona)
- tin_woodman (material provider)

## Required Environments
- emerald_city_palace_exterior

## Continuity Constraints
- Incremental size and state of the green silk balloon
- Weather conditions shifting across three days

## Beat List
- BT001: Initial fabric preparation and gluing
- BT002: Expansion of the balloon structure
- BT003: Integration of heating materials
- BT004: Final assembly of basket and mechanism
field_values.dialogue_relevance: dialogue supports scene intent and continuity
